channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-13889] mechanism to allocate new HSM archive ID
item.description:
Currently, PCC requires that the administrator track and assign the archive IDs for each client with PCC configured. This may be OK for traditional HSM archives (of which there may only be 2-3 for a filesystem), but with PCC this is prone to error, and duplicate archive IDs may result in data loss if the MDS tries to retrieve the archived files from the wrong client.

It would be useful if the MDT tracked archive ID assignment for the PCC archives, so that admins could configure PCC without having to track this themselves. It can probably just be an integer that is incremented, since we now allow up to 2^32 archive IDs in a single filesystem.
item.summary: mechanism to allocate new HSM archive ID

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment: This ticket, like LU-19540 and LU-18659 would benefit from a centralized "ID allocation service" on MDT0000 that can assign sequential IDs for a specific "type" (HSM Archive ID, Project ID, client ID).
It would be good of creation of a new id and assigning it to the parent directory of the archive was an atomic operation, but  the separate operations could just be wrapped in a library function.

rread, there should probably be two separate functions - one to request a new unique ID, and one to assign the ID to the directory (which doesn't exist today).

To implement llapi_assign_archive(const char *path, __u32 id) likely needs several components:

  • a mechanism to track assigned IDs persistently on MDT0000 (or on other MDTs in the future)
  • an interface for clients to request the new ID from the service (e.g. GET_INFO or SET_INFO RPC?)
  • new kernel API to assign the HSM archive ID to a directory and store it on the MDT

For clients that are in a nodemap, the assigned IDs should be mapped into the nodemap ID space (LU-18775), and be unique within the client nodemap, and the filesystem ID should be unique across the whole filesystem.

It would be nice to have an api  like llapi_assign_archive("/mnt/lustre/bucket-dir/"), and then a new archive id is allocated and assigned to that directory.  If there are existing subdirectories then it might be helpful to recursively add the archive id, or that could be done the same time that the files are archived. 

Also, ideally the archive_id would be automatically added to new files and subdirectories created in a directory with an archive_id. (Just like project ids.)

This could potentially be a generic "ID allocation" mechanism that used the ID for both HSM archive as well as project IDs, and they would be (by convention?) the same. This would be a convenient way to track space usage for files in the filesystem using that project == HSM archive (possibly by name once LU-13335 is landed).

The projid and HSM archive ID would still need to be stored separately, in case a file is moved out of one project directory into another project directory and the projid is changed, so that it is possible to restore the file from the right HSM archive.

This would be useful for attaching multiple ephemeral archives to a filesystem (e.g. S3 or GCP buckets) so that there is not a collision between buckets assigned to a specific archive. Ideally, regular users would be able to attach an S3 bucket to a subdirectory that they own to facilitate data import and/or backup.
